data_out.description: Simulation figures and tables demonstrating ORA pitfalls and best practices in metabolomics

domain_knowledge: Over-representation Analysis (ORA) is a pathway analysis method in metabolomics that identifies statistically significant metabolite pathway associations from experimental datasets.
Reproducibility in computational metabolomics requires exact matching of Python version and all dependency versions to ensure simulation outputs are identical to published results.
Jupyter notebooks in metabolomics research serve as both executable documentation and reproducible computational workflows, requiring sequential cell execution to maintain data flow.
Pitfalls in ORA include multiple testing correction strategies, background metabolite database selection, and pathway definition choices that significantly impact result interpretation.

direct_checks: verify file exists: src/reproducible_simulations.ipynb in repository github:cwieder__metabolomics-ORA
file_format_is: src/reproducible_simulations.ipynb is a valid Jupyter notebook (.ipynb)
script_runs: Jupyter notebook executes without errors using Python 3.8
verify notebook completes without exceptions or missing dependencies
verify notebook produces at least one figure or table output artifact
expert_review: outputs (figures and tables) match in content and visual structure those reported in DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1009105
numerical results in simulation outputs are consistent with the article's reported pitfalls and best-practice demonstrations for ORA in metabolomics

expected_outputs: Simulation figures and tables demonstrating ORA pitfalls and best practices in metabolomics
finding: The study provides Python code in a Jupyter notebook (src/reproducible_simulations.ipynb) that users can adapt to perform simulations, with the code tested using Python 3.8 on MacOS with standard hardware.
methodology_summary: Clone the cwieder/metabolomics-ORA repository to obtain the reproducible simulation notebook and dependencies.
Install Python 3.8 and all required packages specified in the repository environment configuration.
Execute the Jupyter notebook src/reproducible_simulations.ipynb cell-by-cell to run ORA simulations.
Collect all generated figures and tables demonstrating ORA pitfalls and best-practice recommendations.
Validation: regenerated outputs match figures and tables published in DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1009105.
missing_information: No changelog documenting version history, modifications, or dependencies of the repository

task_description: Execute the reproducible_simulations.ipynb Jupyter notebook from the cwieder/metabolomics-ORA GitHub repository using Python 3.8 to regenerate simulation outputs (figures and tables) that demonstrate pitfalls and best-practice recommendations for Over-representation Analysis (ORA) in metabolomics.
task_objective: Execute src/reproducible_simulations.ipynb using Python 3.8 to regenerate all simulation outputs (figures and tables) documenting ORA pitfalls and best practices in metabolomics as published in DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1009105.
title: Reproduce the ORA simulation results reported in the study

workflow_description: 1. Clone the cwieder/metabolomics-ORA repository from GitHub. 2. Set up a Python 3.8 environment with all required dependencies listed in the repository. 3. Launch Jupyter and open src/reproducible_simulations.ipynb. 4. Execute all cells in the notebook sequentially to regenerate simulation outputs. 5. Verify that generated figures and tables match those reported in the publication (DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1009105).
